title: NSAID GI risk review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.
red_flags.rationale: The exact alarm section was reviewed for GI-bleeding and dyspepsia concern context; no separate automatic conclusion or action is mapped.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

source.title: Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease and dyspepsia in adults: investigation and management

medication.text: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.
medication.rationale: The exact structured-review section supports NSAID indication, exposure, adherence, benefits, harms, GI symptoms, concurrent medicines, monitoring, and patient priorities without calculating risk or changing treatment.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

medication.text: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.
medication.rationale: The exact involvement section supports clinician-entered risk discussion, plan, safety net, and follow-up without recommending a medicine change.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

source_limitations: NSAID identity, dose, duration, indication, ulcer or bleeding history, concurrent medicines, renal status, calculated risk, medicine decision, test, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
